question: What is the difference between MHC class I and MHC class II molecules?
options: MHC I presents to CD4+ T cells, MHC II to CD8+ T cells
MHC I presents to CD8+ T cells, MHC II to CD4+ T cells
They present the same peptides to the same cells
MHC I is only on B cells, MHC II on all cells
explanation: MHC class I molecules present endogenous peptides to CD8+ cytotoxic T cells, while MHC class II molecules present exogenous peptides to CD4+ helper T cells.
